entry.title: How to extend 500 GL codes to 4 new company codes
entry.content:
Hello team

We have recently set up 4 new company codes and I need to copy over 500 GL Codes to the new companies.
I appreciate that I can do this manually, but it is 2000 transactions! 
Being new to SAP S4 Hana private cloud addition I would expect there is a way to "upload" these entries for the each Company code?

My initial research is that Migration Cockpit could have been a solution, but I do not seem to have the ability to create new projects (Is this a permissions thing?). However, it appears as the data is created for the GL codes this might not be the correct tool.

Is anyone able to advise the best way to do this please?

 

Thanks

Ian

entry.title: Re: New End User File Upload in SAP Analytics Cloud QRC3 2024
entry.content:
Hello  @gerd_schoeffl 

The import works as long as the data is not locked and the columns respect the master data.
Instead of returning a reject file, the import now terminates with error 1043.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Add digital signature in Purchase order form in sap p...
entry.content:
Hi @Ranjan__Panda,

I would recommend exploring the SAP S/4HANA for Enterprise Contract Management (1XV) scope item, as it allows you to sending legal documents for e-signature. Please note that this functionality requires additional license.

Hope this helps.

Arturo Montes de Oca

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to enable custom annotations for CDS L...
entry.content: However this annotation structure can be quite structured since we have different objects and their properties. So I think about ability to design those types with typescript and somehow attach them to CDS extension ( even via compiled files like json schemas ) that's how the extension would be aware of my extension types.

entry.title: ABAP Core Development Tools 3.42.0 vs ABAP Core Development Tools 3.46.0
entry.content:
Dear Community, do you know how to contact with the developers of ADT?
the ABAP Core Development Tools 3.46.0 has no option to enter service key
However the previous one ABAP Core Development Tools 3.42.0
It had a way to enter your service key
Compare these 2 print screen

I am not able to connect to my BTP Trial account since then.

Is there a way to downgrade the tools or correct the new ones?

entry.title: Re: Attachment handling in CPI
entry.content: we also describe how to create atatchments, see https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/create-attachments

entry.title: Re: New End User File Upload in SAP Analytics Cloud QRC3 2024
entry.content: @gerd_schoeffl , Yeah looks like it started working for us. I have not tested for any reject files but a proper load file is getting loaded as normal.

entry.title: Re: SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 Early Adopter Care Program
entry.content: Whats the plan for Lumira?

entry.title: Configure print service entitlements
entry.content:
Hello everyone.
I'm following this guide https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/printing-guide/subscribing-to-sap-print-service?locale=en-US
but opening the 'Subaccount: Digital Manufacturing Cloud - Entitlements' section I can't edit Entitlements 'print service' to add the standard plan as indicated by the guide.
I can't find the Configure Entitlements item.

Any ideas?
thanks in advance.

Mario

entry.title: Re: How to extend 500 GL codes to 4 new company codes
entry.content: Are transactions such as OBY2, OB_GLACC01 or FS15/FS16 available on your version?

entry.title: Re: Configure print service entitlements
entry.content: Same problem here! Is the guide outdated, or are we just missing some permissions?

entry.title: Activate Certification Next Exam Update
entry.content: When will a new version of the Activate Certification exam be released?

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA for product Compliance NEW Dangerous Goods Ma...
entry.content: Hi - I believe I now solved it after investigating the configuration in more detail. The following must be set in the technical job definitions in the config nodes: SAP_EHFND_PCEP, SAP_EHFND_PC_CONTENT_LOAD, SAP_EHFND_PC_SRVREQ_REFRESH On SAP_EHPMA_SVT. This was missed

entry.title: SAP Mobile, SEGW, /IWBEP/CX_QRL_DELTA_UNAVAILABL HTTP410
entry.content:
Good afternoon,

we have implemented an ODATA service using SEGW with delta tracking. Sometimes, when the clients delta_token is too old, the /IWBEP/CX_QRL_DELTA_UNAVAILABL exception occurs. Unfortunately that exception would be responsed with HTTP code 500 which could not be handled be the requesting client.

We opened an SNOTE to Walldorf and asked what we could do. Walldorf answered we should overwrite the HTTP 500 code with an HTTP 410 code when the exception occurs. But - how would you do that in the _DPC_EXT-Class?

Anybody an idea? 

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Parameter Transactions and Last Change dates
entry.content:
For query the first user to execute a modified query will recreate the program

(Did you look for the program in Object Directory Entry)

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to enable custom annotations for CDS L...
entry.content:
Hi @pepl,

I see, that seems to be a good idea.

So you imagine something like cds deploy --to abap, right? I'm not familiar with the structure and data content of abapgit / gcts files, but I'm quite sure that a deterministic mapping could be created for cap schema -> abapgit / gcts. I'm not sure if it is possible to add such thing without modifing the cds library itself, but knowing that exact mapping the conversion is possible, and I can imagine a VSCode extension to do such task.

Regards, Peter

entry.title: Do we have a whatsApp group or telegram group for discussions on SAP HR/HCM?
entry.content:
I am beginner in SAP ERP HCM and looking for resources to learn sap HCM modules like payroll and timemanagement. SAP community is a wonderful resource. Any other i should refer to? Do we have any good whatsapp or telegram group for discussions around SAP HCM/HR? Any leads appreciated.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Export Conditions from mail
entry.content: Hi Joyca, I assume you use the Legacy Editor. See https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MARKETING_CLOUD/b88f770e4b7c4ecead5477e7a6c7b8f7/9cac00cfd1394ab4bd336688bf6028ca.html?locale=en-US "Condition rules for new conditions cannot be downloaded for security reasons." Kind regards, Silvia

entry.title: Re: Export Conditions from mail
entry.content: Hi Joyca, I assume you use the Legacy Editor. See https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MARKETING_CLOUD/b88f770e4b7c4ecead5477e7a6c7b8f7/9cac00cfd1394ab4bd336... "Condition rules for new conditions cannot be downloaded for security reasons." Kind regards, Silvia

entry.title: Employee unable to select a rating
entry.content:
Hi all,

We recently launched new performance forms which are now in the employee assessment stage.

There is currently one employee who is not able to select a self-rating in the form. The option to open the drop-down list is missing:

The weird thing is that if I proxy to her user, and open the form. I am able to select a rating from the dropdown list. It then displays like this:

We already tried logging in and out, we used another browser, but nothing is working. We also launched the form again, but that didn't work as well. 

Did anyone have this issue before and knows how to solve it?

entry.title: Formatting error when printing from an ERP to Zebra Label Printer
entry.content:
Hello! For the past few weeks I've been trying to get a series of Crystal Reports I've made to print to a Zebra label printer on a seperate server using an ERP software (Sage X3). Currently I'm using the ERP to access the Crystal Report, which is printing to dimensions assigned by a "print stock" on the label printer (this is the only way I could get labels to print in the correct orientation for whatever reason).

Every part of this process seems to work exactly as intended aside from one weird issue. All of my reports print from the Crystal print interface exactly as intended, but the second I upload them to the ERP and print from that interface, the prints seem to be forced to only take up a 3x2 space on the label (some of them can range up to 4x6, so it's just at tiny space in the corner). I can turn off "Dissociate Formatting Size from Printer Paper Size", but then it prints correctly height-wise, but gets cut off horizontally. 

I found through testing that 3x2 is the default dimensions for Zebra printers, so my guess is that it has something to do with Crystal not recognizing the stock when it's being printed through an ERP, but I have no clue!

Please help!

-Jack

entry.title: Re: What are reasons for TRDIR-UDAT getting updated apart...

Hi Ryan,

Thanks for the suggestion!

I just checked but there don't seem to be any enhancements in the include or overall program SAPMF02D.

Cheers

Bärbel

@thomas_jung

entry.title: Kanji Characters not visible when using Microsoft Edge Browser with FIORI App ME23N
entry.content:
Hello,

 

SAP recommends the MS Edge Browser when working with S/4HANA FIORI Apps.
When we use the Print Preview of a Purchase Order, containing Kanji Characters, with the FIORI App ME23N in MS Edge, the the Kanji Characters are there, but not visible.
Doing the same with Firefox Browser the Characters are visible.

entry.title: Re: assistance needed in building SAP HANA Cloud and CAP...
entry.content:
See the installation instructions here if you are developing locally: Jumpstart & Grow as You Go | capire

Install the @SAP/cds module globally (-g switch). 

After installing be sure to restart your terminal as well.

entry.title: Re: Kanji Characters not visible when using Microsoft Edg...

entry.title: Re: How to deregister an account from SAP Community?
entry.content:
Please go to https://community.sap.com/t5/user/myprofilepage/tab/personal-profile and select "close account."

Kind regards,

--Jerry

entry.title: Re: trace all queries coming for specific database
entry.content:
Yes, you can and below is the syntax.

sp_audit "all","all",<database_name>,"on"

entry.title: Re: Urgent - handling units are missing in vekp/vepo tables but available in document flow of a deli
entry.content: if anyone faced same issue, please let me know how to solve this.

entry.title: Re: On iPhone the redraw method is not working for all Co...
entry.content:
This is an expected behavior.  On Form Cell controls, a redraw does not fully recreate the control due to the fact that there may be data already entered in the control.  

If you need to show / hide a form cell field you are probably better off getting the field and calling setVisible(false) on it.  If you really want to recreate the form cell field you can get the field and call reset() on it to recreate / redraw which would trigger rule evaluation again for the Visible property.  This will also clear any user input and revert back to the initial value for the field.

entry.title: Re: Kanji Characters not visible when using Microsoft Edg...
entry.content: I did as well. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: readiness check not performed
entry.content:
Hi @catano, this works, but the readiness check add or remove the instance to the cf router. The health check restart the instance ... different behaivior.

command in repo: cf set-health-check cls-srv http --endpoint "/odata/v4/demo/hello(name='world')"

entry.title: Re: Workflow for z report
entry.content:
Did you remember how was the solution for return (yes or No) from email and to process the WF?

I have a requirement Where the user can Approval or Reject the task WF by email  as well. So I need to get the return email(yes or no) in a new Z program and start the task for approval or reject in the WF. I don't know how from the new Z program run the task that I need in that point.

Thank you for any help you can offer.

entry.title: SAP Datasphere - how to externally trigger replication/data flow
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We wanted to trigger Replication flow or Data flow externally via API call through a different scheduling tool (that runs other jobs in the client landscape).
Datasphere has its own scheduling and monitoring tool but we wanted to leverage the current client scheduling tool to run and monitor the flows. Is there a way to do this?

Thanks,

entry.title: How to Make a Field Visible Only When a Specific Option is Selected in SAP Build Process Automation?
entry.content:
Hello,

I am working on a form in SAP Build Process Automation and need help with conditional field visibility. I have a selection field called "Escolha um Veículo" (Choose a Vehicle) with three options: "Carro" (Car), "Moto" (Motorcycle), and "Outro" (Other).

Below this field, I have another field called "Outro Veículo" (Other Vehicle), which should only appear if the user selects the "Outro" option.

I would like to know how to make the "Outro Veículo" text field visible only when the "Outro" option is selected, and hidden otherwise.

Could you please guide me on how to achieve this using SAP Build Process Automation scripting or rules?

Thank you for your help!

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA Installation Steps (Part 1)
entry.content:
Replying to one of the above questions. 

SAP does support running multiple SAP HANA systems (SIDs) on a single production SAP HANA hardware installation. This is restricted to single host / scale-up scenarios only.


Please keep in mind that multi-SID requires significant attention to various detailed tasks related to system administration and performance management.
SAP does support running multiple SAP HANA systems (SIDs) on a single non-production (DEV, QA, test, production fail-over, etc) single node or scale-out SAP HANA environment. For more information, see SAP Note 1681092.
The installation path, data path, and log path must not point to the same directory. A SAP HANA system cannot be installed under /usr/sap/. The directory /usr/sap must not be shared across other hosts. For production systems with high availability, it is possible to share some temporarily unused resources from the standby hosts.
As soon as the standby resources are needed, they must become exclusively 20 PUBLIC SAP HANA Server Installation and Update Guide Concepts and Requirements for an SAP HANA System available for the production system and no longer shared.

For more details, refer to the high availability information in the SAP HANA Administration Guide. Directory names inside the data path and log path will not show the database ID for single-container systems that were converted to tenant database systems. /hana/shared/ must be mounted with the option suid

entry.title: Enabling and Configuring Employee Referral Program
entry.content:
Hello - curious if any other SAP SF Recruiting customers have enabled the Employee Referral Program. If so, did you encounter any issues with accessing the Admin Center>Set Up Employee Referral Program? In provisioning we have setup the company settings to enable the ERP and added the ERP Payout as a currency field in the XML for job requisition. I have all the applicable RBP assigned to my role but I'm not able to access the admin center ERP and the ERP section is not an available option in Edit Status Configuration. 
We're missing something and cannot figure out what it is. I feel like a job or backend sync may need to happen but that's a guess. I've referred to the SAP Help Portal Enabling and Configuring ERP. Any feedback or guidance is appreciated.

entry.title: Como lançar CT-e Substituto modelo 57 no SAP
entry.content:
Boa tarde a todos, a partir de 2025 será validado no SPED a chave de referência do CT-e original que é enviada no xml do CT-e Substituto (Tipo de CT-e = 3) . Tag da chave de referência <infCteSub>.

Temos vários processos para lançar o CT-e modelo 57 e 67 normal. Através do Débito Posterior referente a um pedido de Compras, Criação de PO e MIRO para CT-e modelo 67 e NF writer para registro de CT-e com várias NF´s. 

Para o CT-e Substituto pensei em fazer via writer e no campo "documento original" colocar o docnum do CT-e original, porém os usuários estão nos falando que nem sempre lançam o Ct-e original no SAP.

Alguém recebe CT-e Substituto ? Se sim,  qual o processo que utiliza no SAP?

Obrigada

Elsa

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: OData Connector for PowerBi
entry.content:
Hi @osudfuegd,

We understand the that the connector has its limitations. Currently we in SAP Datasphere are exploring better approaches for PoweBI consumption, as soon as we can we will share more details.

Best Regards
Gustavo Kath

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: OData Connector for PowerBi
entry.content:
Hi @Lora0121,

As far I know only this sample connector available for customer reference and enhancement is available for consuming analytic models and its features. As I mentioned on my previous comment we are currently working on exploring other better possibilities for PowerBI integration, as soon as we can we will share more details.

Best Regards
Gustavo Kath

entry.title: Re: How to see Packing Instruction POP2 Change History
entry.content:
Hello @Mrak1 

I don't think that change documents are supported for packing instructions. They are for EWM packing specification, but I've never seen change documents for POP2.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: New XPATH 2.0 functions in XSLT/XPATH with SAP BTP ABAP Environment 2411
entry.content:
The XML and XSLT support was part of the ABAP programming language for over twenty years. Not much changed in the meantime. With ABAP Environment 2411 or ABAP release 913 three new functions from the XSLT/XPATH 2.0 specification have been added to deal with string replacements and regular expressions.

1. Introduction

For ages, ABAP has supported XSLT 1.0 but includes some functions and instructions from the XSLT 1.1 and XSLT 2.0 specifications, for example, for-each-group from the XSLT 2.0 specification.

Powerful functions for string replacements and matching, however, have been missing from the ABAP implementation. These are crucial for a language like XSLT, which is used to transform XML documents. This seriously limited the usefulness of XSLT in ABAP.

In 2411 we introduce the XPATH functions matches, replace in the default namespace and sap:tokenize in the SAP namespace. These behave as specified in the official W3C specification XQuery 1.0 and XPath 2.0 Functions and Operators.

This was made possible by the introduction of XPATH regular expression dialect in ABAP in SAP Basis 782 / SAP S/4 HANA CE 2011, see the excellent blog post of Julius in Modern Regular Expressions in ABAP – Part 3. In that release, XPATH regular expressions have only been allowed via the ABAP class CL_ABAP_REGEX and not in XPATH/XSLT itself. This is now to be changed.

Note that the regular expression dialect used for these functions is the dialect specified in the W3C specification and not the usual PCRE or POSIX regular expressions used more often in ABAP itself. 

2. The function "matches()"

The XPATH function matches() is used to find a regular expression inside a string.

Purpose: Find regular expression in a string
Arguments:  
    1. string to be searched
    2. string containing the regular expression
    3. (optional) string with options (case insensitive search, ...)
Return type: boolean

Consider the following XML document:

<pets>
  <dog name="Cat" color="grey"/>
  <cat name=" Cat" color="white"/>
  <cat name="Cat" color="orange"/>
  <cat name="Cot" color="brown"/>
  <cat name="CAT Garfield" color="purple"/>
  <cat name="The Cat" color="pink"/>
</pets>

Consider the following XSLT snippet applied to the above XML document:

<xsl:for-each select="/pets/cat[matches(@name, '^C.t')]">
  <pet>
    <xsl:attribute name="name"><xsl:value-of select="@name"/></xsl:attribute>
    <xsl:attribute name="color"><xsl:value-of select="@color"/></xsl:attribute>
  </pet>
</xsl:for-each>

This will just return the lines:

<pet name="Cat" color="orange"/>
<pet name="Cot" color="brown"/>

Changing the matches-call to matches(@name, '^C.t', 'i') will do a case-insensitive search instead.

3. The function "replace()"

The replace function is similar to matches() but does a replacement. The replacement string can refer to submatches of the regular expression, as is known from similar functionality in ABAP or, e.g. vscode.

Purpose: Replace all non-overlapping occurrences of the regular expression in the search string with a replacement string
Arguments:  
    1. string to be searched
    2. string containing the regular expression
    3. string containing the replacement
    4. (optional) string with options (case insensitive search, ...)
Return type: string with modifications

Note that the syntax of the replacement string in the XPATH standard is very weird in corner cases. Please look that up in the W3C specification if you are confused. 

Using the same XML as above, and the following XSLT script (note we do case-insensitive replacement)!

<xsl:for-each select="/pets/cat[matches(@name, '^C.t')]">
  <pet>
    <xsl:attribute name="name">
      <xsl:value-of select="replace(@name, '^C(.)t', '$1', 'i')"/>
    </xsl:attribute>
    <xsl:attribute name="color"><xsl:value-of select="@color"/></xsl:attribute>
  </pet>
</xsl:for-each>

returns the following result:

<pet name="a" color="orange"/>
<pet name="o" color="brown"/>
<pet name="A Garfield" color="purple"/>

4. The function tokenize()

The tokenize() function splits a string at separators, which can be given via a regular expression. 

Purpose: Split a string at a separator regular expression
Arguments:  
    1. string to be searched
    2. string containing the regular expression
    3. (optional) string with options (case insensitive search, ...)
Return type: node-set of XML text nodes consisting of the split tokens 

The function tokenize() is special, as it returns a sequence of strings in the XPATH standard. A sequence is a datatype introduced with XPATH/XSLT 2.0 and does not exist in the XPATH implementation available in ABAP. So we changed the return type to return a node-set, i.e. a set of unrelated XML nodes consisting only of single strings. This behaves in all practical purposes like the standard tokenize() function. Nevertheless, the function is only exposed in the SAP namespace. 

Note there exists a one-argument version of tokenize() with special semantics. If you are interested, look it up in the standard.

We again use the same XML as above and apply the following XSLT snippet:

<xsl:for-each select="/pets/cat">
  <pet>
    <xsl:attribute name="name"><xsl:value-of select="@name"/></xsl:attribute>
    <xsl:attribute name="position"><xsl:value-of select="position()"/>
    </xsl:attribute>
    <xsl:for-each select="sap:tokenize(@name, '\s+')">
      <token>
        <xsl:copy-of select="."/>
      </token>
    </xsl:for-each>
  </pet>
</xsl:for-each>

This will return:

<pet name=" Cat" position="1">
  <token>Cat</token>
</pet>
<pet name="Cat" position="2">
  <token>Cat</token>
</pet>
<pet name="Cot" position="3">
  <token>Cot</token>
</pet>
<pet name="CAT Garfield" position="4">
  <token>CAT</token>
  <token>Garfield</token>
</pet>
<pet name="The Cat" position="5">
  <token>The</token>
  <token>Cat</token>
</pet>

5. Conclusion

This article introduced the new regular expression functions: matches, replace and tokenize in ABAP XSLT, which are the first new functions in ABAP XSLT for decades.

entry.title: Create Worksheet for new manager(s) does not work in Update All Worksheet
entry.content:
Hi @xavierlegarrec 

We are facing a wired issue with Create Worksheet for new manager(s) option. The issue is happening with Total Compensation form, but works fine with Compensation form.

We have already launched the forms, and have scheduled Updated Compensation Forms for Template job with settings apart from others:

* Create worksheet for new manager - we have given worksheet name and head of planning ID.

* Move compensation data from old to new manager

. But new worksheet for new manager is not getting created. The error says 'WARNING - Unable to move the user XXX from the old manager form BBPlanning OldManagername to the new manager form to reflect manager change, because the new manager NewManagername either has no form or has multiple forms. '

Thanks,

Swagatika

entry.title: Re: SD self-billing - SAFT
Re: SD self-billing - SAFT
entry.content: Hello, First, thank you for the answer and help. We are currently analyzing Chapter 10.2.2. When we try to classify this document as an external document, we are required to migrate certain original information into SAP, such as the document date, document number, and digital signature. However, in our case, we do not have this information. For instance, the end customer may not have a digital signature, especially in situations where the end customer is not a Portuguese. We believe the "external document" classification only applies if the document is part of software owned by another company in the group, which is not the case for us. Best regards, Ana

entry.title: Cloud Integration requirement
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Is the below scenario possible using SAP BTP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration).

Our Integration Suite is hosted on public cloud.. Is the below scenario possible to achieve using SAP BTP Integration Suite? What exactly is needed in order to connect to Kafka SESP platform? What information Should I collect from Sender system? Could you please let me know the steps needed to achieve this requirement? Thank you in advance for your inputs..

 

Thanks

Ramu

entry.title: Re: [BTP Security Latest]: How to Get Current Logged-on User Details in SAP Business Technology Plat
entry.content:
Hi showkath_naseem,
Thanks for the info, im doing the approach 3.5, with a Fiori FreeStyle and a managed AppRouter, i deploy my app in Build WorkZone in CF but i have the next error

The xs-app.json:

    {
      "source": "^/user-api(.*)",
      "target": "$1",
      "service": "sap-approuter-userapi",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa",
      "csrfProtection": false
    },

and this is the code:

_loadUserInfo: function () {
            var oModel = this.getView().getModel("userInfo");
            var sUrl = "/userapi/currentUser"; // or /user-api/attributes

            $.ajax({
                "url": sUrl,
                async: false,
                success: jQuery.proxy(function (user) {
                    var currentUser = {
                        fullName: user.lastName + ", " + user.firstName,
                        userId: user.name,
                    }

                    oModel.setData(currentUser);
                }),
                error: function (error) {
                    MessageToast.show("Error in load.", error);
                }
            });
        }

Everything seems fine to me, is there an additional setting?.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Create Worksheet for new manager(s) does not work in...
entry.content:
Hi @swagatikapanda 

"we have given worksheet name and head of planning ID" : as per discussion on other community chat are you sure you have entered the person who sits at the very top of the orgchart for the planning method selected ? most of the time this is the issue. 

The system needs to create a worksheet first so it can route the employee. If "Create new worksheet for new manager" fails then "Move employee from old to new manager" will fail automatically too.

If you did enter the CEO or whoever sits on top of orgchart in your mass update worksheets job for "Create a form for new manager" then please create a support ticket as I won't be able to help more, it should work.

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: SAP HEC vs SAP RISE
Re: Create a Fiori Application using RESTful Application programming model(RAP) on S/4 HANA cloud pl
entry.content: At the time creating entries, it is showing be blank page with create and cancel option

entry.title: Re: Welcome to Career Corner
entry.content:
Melissa,
Hi! Your time with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition will be very useful. There is a demand for this expertise at all levels (beginner, intermediate, expert). 

Here's some "free" SAP S/4HANA Cloud courses. Just click on the course and look under the title for the cost. I see some are "free" and others are "free until certification." 

All the best!
Rich

entry.title: Missing settlement rules for Service Documents - Profitability Segment
entry.content:
The distribution rules from service document are missing in the "Manage Settlement Rules - Service Documents", but they should be derived from Profitability Segment.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Missing settlement rules for Service Documents - Prof...
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,


In order to derive the Profitability Segment in the distribution rule, you can double click the Account Assignment Cat., which will open the details for the rule. There, you will have the Profitability Segment button, that you can click and select 'Continue'. This will allow you to create your derived distribution rules.

Manage Settlement Rules - Service Documents


Best Regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Audit data and Configuration changes in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hello Hari,

I have some questions related to this Audit report:

1. you have mentioned "You could always track the status of the job under the Access Reports Tab. You would receive an email with the report download link." 

we are not receiving any email with download link. Is there any setting for this?

2.There is an limitation for the by user search upto 10. How can be check for more than 10 or multiple users who have done the changes. Can table report helps us?

entry.title: PwC webinar on SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
entry.content:
With the increasing number of mandatory e-invoicing and e-reporting obligations in Europe, it is crucial for businesses to stay proactive in addressing these requirements. Germany (starting January 2025), Belgium (January 2026), Poland (February 2026), and France (September 2026) are among the countries introducing new regulations. This session will highlight how SAP's DRC (Document and Reporting Compliance) can assist businesses in effectively handling these obligations.

You can find more information about the agenda and speakers here. 

To secure your spot, please register here.

entry.title: Periodic Billing with price change.
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have created a Periodic billing plan and am using it. I am generating the billing plan at the item level and using this contract to create invoice, directly.

Now, there is a requirement to have an option to increase the price of each line item, inside the periodic billing plan. Example, inside the periodic billing plan, each line item should increase by 5% compounded, compounded. Is there a way to make the price of each line item inside the Periodic Billig editable or make it incremental?

Please tell  will this be done

entry.title: Re: SAC : Copy Pages to Other Stories When used DWC live Models as Story Source
entry.content: Hello--I've created an Influence request for this situation. Please vote! https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/334757

entry.title: Re: Need help to install ABAP Development Tools plugin in Eclipse
entry.content: Thank you every one. I figured out the issue and able to install the ADT and BW-MT in eclipse

entry.title: Re: 400 Bad HTTP Request
entry.content: You could also verify why the external system returns a non-standard HTTP code... which would be wrong...

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud - Copy one page from a story to a...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thanks for this.  For those trying this with Stories with a Datasphere Live connection, this does not work.  

KBA: https://me.sap.com/notes/3128880 

Here is an Influence request that I have created.  

 

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/334757 

Please vote!

entry.title: How to create Mandatory Variable at Story level in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
I have a requirement to create variables at Story level, when the user opens up/refresh the story, he/she has to see the variable prompt with Mandatory and Optional variables to enter values.

When i try to create a variable, i can see the below.

I can create variables at Model level but unable to create a Story Level.

I searched everywhere, but no luck, Could someone please help me?

entry.title: Copy widgets or pages between Stories when using aDatasphere Live Connection
entry.content:
Hello,

I've learned that it is not possible to copy widgets or pages between Stories when using a Datasphere Live Connection.     

Details in this KBA: https://me.sap.com/notes/3128880 

Here is an Influence request that I have created.  

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/334757 

If this is something that you would find useful, please vote! 

Thank you

SAP Analytics Cloud SAP Datasphere

entry.title: SAP Business Transformation Center - Lean Selective Data Transition
entry.content:
Introduction

You want to transition your SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition in a simple and guided way with no additional costs for tooling?

With its latest major solution increment, the SAP Business Transformation Center completes the well-known Digital Blueprint by offering subsequent data transformation and data migration capabilities. In this way, SAP Business Transformation Center covers almost all six steps of the data rainbow every data transition project follows.

Figure 1 Six Steps of the Data Rainbow

In this blog post, we are going to build on top of the content from the previous blog posts

  • SAP Business Transformation Center - Digital Blueprint
  • Completing the Digital Blueprint - with the System Scan

Be sure to read them first before continuing.

Thus, our focus will be on the execution part of a selective data migration using the SAP Business Transformation Center. We will outline the steps that need to be taken and how the solution provides guidance and facilitates traditional efforts to execute the data transition.

Usage Guide

The prerequisites and the subsequent execution steps for the execution of a Lean Selective Data Transition are described within the following sections.

Prerequisites

Using the SAP Business Transformation Center for a data migration requires starting conditions on source – and target system. Both systems must be SAP systems. A data migration from or to a non-SAP system is currently not supported.

The SAP ECC source system must have minimum release 6.0 with Enhancement Pack 8 and the SAP S/4HANA target system minimum release 2023. Additionally, there are specific components such as ST-PI and DMIS which need to be on a dedicated level. For latest details refer to the Setup and Administration Guide as well as to the Security Guide for SAP Business Transformation Center.

The SAP S/4HANA target system needs to be created based on a Shell-Conversion. A new installation of the SAP S/4HANA target system is not supported.

For further information, please have a look at Lean Selective Data Transition Best Practices.  

Execution Steps

Step 1: Confirm the Digital Blueprint

The Confirmation of a Digital Blueprint represents the sign-off of all relevant scoping discussions and decisions made in the planning phase of a Lean Selective Data Transition. Once done in the Fiori App “Manage Digital Blueprint”, the action is not reversable. In case of potential changes after confirmation, the copy function needs to be utilized. In this way, clear documentation and compliance is ensured.

A pop-up summarizes scoping decisions made and provides hints such as the special consideration of industry specific content or Human Capital Management data.

Figure 2 Example Confirmation of a Digital Blueprint

Optionally, a PDF extraction can be done of the Digital Blueprint for sharing the results with roles having no system access. Only a confirmed Digital Blueprint allows to proceed with the next step, the creation of a Transformation Model for the Digital Blueprint.

Step 2: Create a Transformation Model

After scoping the data to be migrated in the Digital Blueprint, a translation from a business perspective into technical objects is done. This is realized by a Transformation Model for a Digital Blueprint. The generation can be triggered out of the Fiori App “Manage Digital Blueprint” by using the in-app navigation or by directly choosing the Fiori App “Manage Transformation Models”.

It comes without additional configuration or coding. Thus, efforts and error-proneness are heavily reduced which accelerate project activities.

The Transformation Model contains versatile pre-delivered rules and definitions and is enhanced by an automatically triggered Data Dictionary scan of the source system. The purpose of the scan is to retrieve technical details to allow relevant mapping schemes in background. The combination of both - the built-in transformation rules and definitions and the applied mappings - results in a technically consistent model that is represented in the Transformation Objects.

These Transformation Objects contain not only the scoped business entities, but also include technical objects such as change documents or language dependent texts. Deriving these contents sets the foundation for a complete and consistent business data migration. Furthermore, the Transformation Model determines filters for data extraction and lists details such as the table structure and relations per transformation object.

For custom specific transformation objects, you have the option to change the State and the Write Behavior. The State influences if a custom object is included for a subsequent data migration. The write behavior controls how possibly existing entries in the target system are handled. This allows flexibility in data migration projects where different landscapes may be used in various migration cycles.

Figure 3 Example Settings for Custom Objects in the Transformation Model

Make use of the comprehensive in-app help by hitting the question mark icon top right, to explore all contents of the single Fiori Apps in the SAP Business Transformation Center.

Figure 4 Example Transformation Model w/ in-App Help

For auditing purposes, an immutable Transformation Model Version needs to be created. A single Transformation Model can contain multiple versions. It manifests all potential settings made and serves as input for the subsequent step, the creation of a migration cycle for the Transformation Model Version.

Step 3: Create & Execute a Cycle

A Cycle migrates the scoped and translated data into the target system. It its created based on a Transformation Model Version and manages the physical data transfer between source and target, including pre-checks and a detailed monitoring of the execution progress.

By using the Fiori App “Manage Cycles” or directly choosing the in-app navigation from the Transformation Model Version, a cycle is quickly created by maintaining a name, a cycle type as well as retention date and source and target system.

The cycle type aligns with the typical project phases, where data migrations are conducted multiple times. Furthermore, it has impact on the severity of transfer errors. While a test cycle can circumvent specific error messages, a productive cycle would be impeded.

The source and target systems need to be maintained in the Cloud ALM Landscape Management upfront and currently must contain the same client ID.

Figure 5 Example General Data of a Cycle

Once a cycle is successfully created, the execution of the data migration is subdivided in 4 main steps.

Step 3.1. Pre-validation

The purpose of this step is to avoid errors in the actual execution of the data migration. This is reached by validations and safety checks, such as a proper preparation of source and target system based on the set up and administration guide as well as the security guide.

Additionally, the target system needs to be empty to avoid migrating in an already productively used environment.

Figure 6 Example Cycle Pre-validation Results

The pre-validation can be triggered several times for a single cycle to allow fixing potential errors or warnings. Details on failed validations are stored in a dedicated log for the cycle.

Figure 7 Example of Errors in the Cycle Pre-validation Log

Step 3.2. Preparation

Before the data migration can start, the source and target system need to be configured and migration specific run time objects need to be created. This step does not require any manual configuration by the user, it is all done in background and monitored out of the cycle.

Following the known pattern when confirming a Digital Blueprint, a pop-up summarizes relevant information before initiating activities in the source– and target systems.

Figure 8 Example Cycle Preparation Readiness Summary

Once the cycle preparation task reaches a 100% status in the progress bar for source and target system, it’s all set for the start of the physical data migration. The aggregated status of the cycle switches from “Initialize” to “Ready”.

Figure 9 Example Status Overview after Preparation

Step 3.3. Execution

The start of the data migration for a cycle is set in the Fiori App “Run Cycles”. It can be simply accessed via the in-app navigation of Fiori App “Manage Cycles” or directly in the Fiori Launchpad. The app provides the current progress of all transformation objects and their respective migration steps.

The actual migration of the derived transformation objects is either initiated with full scope by using the “Run All” option or by manually selecting “Run Selected”.  A restart is available as well and could be utilized in case or any errors, such as missing authorization for technical users in the target system.

Figure 10 Example Success Message after “Run All”

Details of the progress can now be monitored for each single transformation object in its detail view.

Figure 11 Example Details for a Transformation Object

Fiori App “Manage Cycles” provides a cumulative status overview for the whole execution step of the cycle.

Figure 12 Example Execution Status Overview

Step 3.4. Postprocessing

The successful physical migration sets the start for the last execution step of a cycle within the SAP Business Transformation Center, the conversion of the migrated data into the SAP S/4HANA business data model.

Following the same pattern as within the Run & Monitor step, the postprocessing is started in the Fiori App “Postprocess Cycles”.  It can be accessed via the in-app navigation of Fiori App “Manage Cycles” or directly in the Fiori Launchpad. The App provides overview and status of all relevant postprocessing items.

By hitting the run button, you can choose between stopping at each potential error or doing a total run of all items.  

Figure 13 Example Start of Postprocessing

The overall progress and status can be monitored in the cycle overview.

Figure 14 Example Status Monitoring for Postprocessing

Potential errors are attached to a dedicated log, following the already mentioned pattern above.

Once the postprocessing of a cycle has successfully finished, the overall cycle execution is set to done. There are no more system activities required in the SAP Business Transformation Center.

All subsequent steps of the end-to-end transition such as the Finance Conversion or other application specifics as well as custom code adjustments post-tasks are currently handled outside of the SAP Business Transformation Center.

Step 4: Execute Follow Up Data Transition Tasks

Moving to SAP S/4HANA by a Selective Data Transition does not consist of a data transition only. After the data migration by the SAP Business Transformation Center, there are different tasks to be done in the S/4HANA target system. Among others, the finance migration or latest Fiori set up. Furthermore, a technical ramp up and validation and testing activities will follow. This follow up tasks can be compared to the activities with a system conversion. Guidance on these steps can be found in the Lean Selective Data Transition Best Practices.

Further Information & Outlook

The Lean Selective Data Transition solution increment was publicly announced in May 2024. Further sources of information can be found on the SAP Community Topic Page “Data Transition and Information”, such as upcoming events and partner related content.

We highly recommend the in-depth Demo Video by the Chief Product Owner of the SAP Business Transformation Center.

Being a Cloud-native application, new features are delivered incrementally on a frequent basis. Don’t miss the latest feature deliveries within the What’s New Section of the SAP Business Transformation Center.

Planned future innovations can be found in the SAP Roadmap Explorer.

Please note that this blog represents the current state of the solution based on an example dataset. Therefore, content and appearance may vary in future releases and based on your individual data.

Conclusion

This blog post provides guidance for customers and partners engaging with the recently released Lean Selective Data Transition increment of the SAP Business Transformation Center.  

For more detailed information or to gain a deeper understanding of the solution's functionality, we recommend familiarizing yourself with the additional resources mentioned above.

We value your input and look forward to hearing your feedback, proposals for improvement as well as general thoughts. Please feel free to share your insights in the comment section below or utilize the embedded feedback option directly in the SAP Business Transformation Center.

Figure 15 embedded Feedback Option

entry.title: Re: Inbound Email integration from O365 to SAP S4 via SAP CPI
entry.content: Even though it can be an option going through SAP CPI for inbound emails into SAP on-prem with everything you explained in the blog, there will be cost associated with each of those messages/iflow that will come under non-sap to SAP. Isn't it ? It may be good for SAP, but not for customers who now have additional opex to consider.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, November 7th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/11/07/sap-developer-news-november-7th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

CAP October 2024 release

  • Release notes https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/releases/oct24

New Adaptation Editor & Hands-on SAP Dev live stream series

  • Adaptation Editor: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/introducing-the-new-adaptation-editor-experience-for-sapui5-adaptation/ba-p/13923903
  • Hands-on SAP Dev – Back to Basics – Web Development: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sabpKj-Hf3c

Creating remote data in HDI

  • hdbfabricvirtualtable in HDI: https://community.sap.com/t5/blogs/blogarticleprintpage/blog-id/technology-blog-sap/article-id/176359
  • Step by Step guide to creating remote data in HDI: https://community.sap.com/t5/blogs/blogarticleprintpage/blog-id/technology-blog-sap/article-id/176359
  • Advanced Strategies for Intelligent Data Applications on SAP HANA Cloud from Devtoberfest: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/advanced-strategies-for-intelligent-data-applications-on-sap-hana-cloud/ev-p/13862880

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 CAP October 2024 release

2:25 New Adaptation Editor & Hands-on SAP Dev live stream series

3:38 Creating remote data in HDI

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for November 7th, 2024.

[DJ] Hello, I'm just on my way to Flexso Tech Day 2024 Tomorrow, I'm in my hotel room in Brussels here in Belgium, but I've just got time to tell you about the October 2024 release of CAP. As usual, there's a great set of new features, including the first one about CAPire. you're looking at configuration properties, you can now hover over certain elements and it will show you exactly how and where to make those configurations. In the CDS language and compiler area there's a new type, a new core type, CDS.map. Now this is represented in OData V4 as an empty open complex type and currently is only supported by CAP Java and of course with OData V4 as well. In the Node.js runtime there's an extension to allow the customization of the request limits when making dollar batch requests and in Java there are plenty of changes some important ones one relating to the dependency removal for a deprecated multi-tenancy library there's also some authorization hardening relating to tenant lifecycle endpoints and of course as I've already just mentioned there's new basic support for the new CDS map type which is in beta. There are also in the Java language runtime enhancements for CDS SQL and also enhancements for OData v4. There is also finally in the Java section some new tool support for ams talking of tools there's some general facilities in vs code with the extension for renaming cds files so that you don't accidentally rename files and clobber yourself because you're referencing those files in a using statement what's not to like. Anyway, read all about these October 2024 changes in Capire and I'll see you online.

[Nico] Hi everyone and welcome to the SAP Developer News. I'm here at the Flexso Tech Day 2024 in Belgium together with my good friend DJ and I do have a few Fiori UI5 and web development related updates for you. First of all, the new adaptation editor for the SAP Business Application Studio was just released. It's part of the SAP Fiori tools for the Business Application Studio and comes with some handy new updates and a nice updated experience. Definitely check it out if you are working with adaptation projects. And second of all, I have just started a new Livestream series as part of Hands-on SAP Dev. It's about back to basics for web development. So if you're a web developer or just starting out, we are going back to the basics, covering the absolute fundamentals, and then eventually we're gonna work our way up to more advanced topics, such as UI5, UI elements, and all that good stuff. And also the SAP Cloud Application Program Model. So we're covering both front-end and back-end. Definitely check it out, and I hope to see you there. Bye.

[Witalij] Are you working with the HANA deployment infrastructure? The HDB Fabric Virtual Table plugin in HDI, introduced earlier this year, enables you to add a system-owned replica to a virtual table from a design time fabric virtual table. If you're unsure what it means, please check the Devtoberfest session, Advanced Strategies for Intelligent Data Applications on SAP HANA Cloud, where product manager, Volker Sago, explained and demoed it. Now, his recently published blog post, step-by-step guide for creating remote data in HDI provides a comprehensive walkthrough for setting up remote data in an HDI container on SAP HANA Cloud. It begins with the preparation phase, which includes obtaining and importing the necessary certificates for secure connections. The next step involves defining the remote source using the appropriate configurations and credentials. The guide also covers creating a custom user provided service or CUPS to grant the necessary rights to HDI containers. Additionally, it explains how to define virtual tables within the HDI container. This detailed guide is essential for technical users looking to integrate remote data sources into the SAP HANA Cloud environment efficiently.

entry.title: Re: No RFC authorization for function module RFCPING.
entry.content:
Yes, as I said: transaction SMGW, usually under "Goto --> Expert Functions --> External Security"
Depending on the release, there are buttons to edit the reginfo and secinfo configuration files.
(secinfo is not needed for this case.)

entry.title: PMU-20173 - Setting Up Single Sender for Email Notifications creates RMK problem
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm writing about PMU-20173. This new feature solves one problem, but creates a bigger problem. This will solve the problem of performance emails coming from an unrecognizable email address and going to spam or being reported as phishing. This can still be an unmonitored email box because SF PMGM will place the responses into the PMGM module. 

However, since the same single email address is now required to be used in RMK, all automated external recruiting emails will now come from the same address used for performance management. This is a MAJOR problem. Currently, our automated RMK emails are coming from an email box monitored by Talent Acquisition. PMU-20173 now forces us to change those RMK emails to come from another box intended for PMGM, time sheet notifications, PTO notifications, etc.

So, lets look further at solutions:

1. Use an unmonitored email for RMK which will also be used for PMGM and all other notifications. The downside to this is a decrease in the ease of communications for job applicants. A question I have for SAP is, will the job applicant replying to the unmonitored box, get an email kickback saying the email is not valid?

2.  Use a monitored email for PMGM and RMK. The people monitoring that inbox will receive correspondence from candidates, but they would also get highly sensitive performance review information. This is not viable. 

Another question for SAP: We currently have this enabled in prod but it doesn't impact RMK and PMGM notifications. What happens to our current RMK notifications, PMGM notifications, and other notifications if we disable the single sender after 2H2024

Thank you

entry.title: Hello ALL,I am new in UI5.Do you have any example of Function imports using SAPiu5
Re: SAP Business Transformation Center - Lean Selective Data Transition
entry.content: Thank you, Matthias, for this informative blog post! It provides a clear and detailed look into the capabilities of the SAP Business Transformation Center and the Lean Selective Data Transition approach. I hope it encourages many readers to explore the benefits of this solution for their own SAP S/4HANA migration journeys. Great work!

entry.title: Re: Start Your scripting Journey The Easy Way With SAP Analytics Cloud | Part Two
entry.content:
Great blog, @Daniel_Davis . Really enjoying the content in this series.


One thing I've really been impressed with is the general layout of your dashboard pages. Do you plan on including any content in this series covering your layout design tips? I exclusively use the 'Optimized Design Experience' for Responsive stories, but often have trouble when it comes to widget alignment and making sure everything looks symmetrical. This is then compounded when I view on smaller screen sizes and/or mobile devices. Looking to gain some confidence in this area, and would be greatly interested in your input.

entry.title: How to mass update Sales Contracts items?
entry.content:
Hello,

We have the requirement to update Sales Contracts item quantity in mass, we have too many documents and it's unviable to do it one by one. Unfortunately, this field is not available in the app 'Mass Change to Sales Documents'. Is there a way to do it in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

Kind regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to mass update Sales Contracts items?
entry.content:
Hi,

Yes, you can update Sales Contracts items through API Sales Contract A2X OData V2/4. Check the following documentation:

Sales Contract (A2X, OData V2) | SAP Help Portal
Operations for Sales Contract (A2X) | SAP Help Portal
Update Sales Contract Item | SAP Help Portal

Sales Contract (A2X, OData V4) | SAP Help Portal
Operations for Sales Contracts (A2X, OData V4) | SAP Help Portal
Update Requests | SAP Help Portal

Please note that batch requests may have some constraints on those APIs, please refer to the 'Constraints' section on the above documentations and to this following:
$batch Processing | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Start Your scripting Journey The Easy Way With SAP Analytics Cloud | Part Three
entry.content: Silly question, but how did you make the Reset button so small? Using the responsive pages the smallest I can get buttons is 2x2 grids, which still feels bulky. I assume you are using a Canvas page - are these pages responsive to smaller screen sizes and/or mobile devices?

entry.title: SAP Signavio Process Intelligence will not accept the SQL request under "SQL Filter"
entry.content:
Hello, 

I have been trying to filter <null> statements in a certain column from a table that I have extracted from ServiceNow. I am in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, Source Data>Tables>Configuration Under SQL Filter. I put in the following SQL query.

"SELECT * FROM sc_request WHERE closed_at IS NOT NULL"

Something went wrong

Error: GENERAL_ERROR

Error Id: df52c106-37e5-43f8-b68d-2cc9a649f506
Landscape: prod_cloud_os_us
Must be a valid Select SQL query.

Why doesn't SAP Signavio Process Intelligence accept this query?

Attached screenshot of page.

entry.title: Change text for the Compensation guideline error message
entry.content:
Hi

I've used Manage languages before but I've having trouble finding the "key" for this error message when a planner goes over the guideline. for example - "Total Performance Award of $10,423.60 (11.00%) is outside guideline of 0.00% to 5.50%." See attached for an image of the error message.

If I change my language to english debug it's difficult to get the error message to show up.

entry.title: SAP IPS: Wrong value of schemas attribute
entry.content:
Helo Experts,

When I create the source system as IAS and the target system as SAC, provisioning to SAC works fine initially. However, when I make some changes or copy the code from the SAP documentation for SCIM API 2 and  a few errors appear - "Duplicated target paths warning and I try to resolve the errors, but I am unable to fix them.

Then, when I revert to the original transformation code (the one that worked at the beginning), I reinsert it, and provisioning stops working.

I get this error:

Caused by: Could not process successfully all entities from system with name: Source IAS

error=HTTP operation failed invoking https://tenant.cloud.sap/api/v1/scim2/Users with statusCode: 400, Response: {"schemas":["urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error"],"status":"400","scimType":"invalidValue","detail":"Request is unparsable, syntactically incorrect, or violates schema. Wrong value of schemas attribute"},

content=<hidden content> - Set property ips.trace.failed.entity.content=true to display the content of failed entities

Then nothing works anymore, and I have to delete the systems and create new ones.

Please help me understand how to avoid or fix such errors."

What am I doing wrong?

Many Thanks

entry.title: Your Sherlock Holmes – Why Product-Specification Configuration Errors Out in the Public Cloud?
entry.content:
Introduction

In my previous blog Your Sherlock Holmes - Why a Fiori App Cannot be Found in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?, I discussed the tight binding of business catalogs and business roles and its direct impact to user’s access to the system. While writing a new blog From A to Z: Setup a Starter System of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition [2024 Edition], I experienced another challenge in this regard: I was denied doing business process configuration. Here is the story to share with you.

 

Scenario

Recently I set up two SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition systems, one is the Starter System and another one is the 3 System Landscape (3SL) system. It went smoothly for all scope activation, organization structure setup, and the final milestone confirmation.  However, when I start doing the Product-Specific Configuration, the system takes me to the Customizing Tenant 100 without any problem, but I got the below error message. By the way, I do have the BPC_EXPERT user role assigned.

Error: No authorization to start the UI for transaction S_ER1_83000079

This not just happened to a few business processes, but all of them. What went wrong?

 Configure Your Business Processes in the Central Business Configuration Tenant

 

Investigation

Naturally, I investigated the user authorization in the Customizing Tenant 100. The ZBR_BPC_EXPERT user role was copied from SAP business role template SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT, and it was assigned to me.

Business Role ZBR_BPC_EXPERT

There is one area caught my attention: there are only 109 Business Catalogs in this business role.  In comparison, other systems have 220+ business catalogs for this BPC_EXPERT role. Why do I have only a half of business catalogs?  I can manually do a comparison and add them in. But it is tedious.

One of my colleagues asked me a critical question at the time: when did you create your ZBR_BPC_EXPERT business role?  From my vague memory, I remembered I did the business role creation after the CBC milestone was confirmed. Is my memory correct?

The business role ZBR_BPC_EXPERT change log (by click on Display Changes button in the above figure) shows the role was created on October 22, 2024 at 18:20:04.

 Change Log of the Business Role ZBR_BPC_EXPERT

To find out when the business catalogs became available, I need to check the CBC Staging transport.  By opening the Fiori app Export Customizing Transport, it lists three transports. The oldest one G6IK900007 is for initial configuration. That is the one I am looking for.

 Fiori app Export Customizing Transport

Opening the transport G6IK900007, then click on Display CBC Staging button, we can see exactly when this transport content was created.

Note: on this screen, it shows Changed on 11/05/2204, because I did a Check on this transport. So, this time stamp can no longer provide the creation date information I am looking for.  That prompts me to go deeper to find out the original transport creation date and time by clicking the Display CBC Staging button.

 Details of Transport G6IK900007

The CBC Staging screen displays the detailed time information I am looking for.  This staging transport was created on 10/23/2024 at 15:30:25, and completed (another Changed) on 10/23/2024, at 20:56:14.

Note: In my blog From A to Z: Setup a Starter System of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition [2024 Edition], I mentioned it took more than six hours for the milestone confirmation to complete. The above timestamp indicates the transport creation took 5 hours 26 minutes.

 BC Staging Information

Now I found the cause of the problem: I created the business role ZBR_BPC_EXPERT one day earlier than the CBC Staging transport was created. In other words, not all the needed business catalogs were available at that time. That’s why ZBR_BPC_EXPERT only contains 109 available business catalogs, not 200+.

Here is a quick explanation on what Milestone Confirmation (activity Confirm Scope and Organizational Structure Phase is Completed) does on the CBC tenant. After you activate the scopes, set up the organization structure, you confirm the milestone. At this step, it transfers all the necessary information based on your configuration from the CBC to the S/4 Customizing Tenant 100, all needed business catalogs and business role templates become visible. Subsequently, the Customizing Tenant 100 puts all this configuration information, including business catalogs and business role templates into the BP staging transport so that they can be transported to the Test Tenant and Production Tenant subsequently. On a new Customizing Tenant 100 before this BC staging transport is created due to milestone confirmation, there are only a limited number of business catalogs and business role templates. 

In our case, the business role template SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT was available but only contains 109 business catalogs. The number of business catalogs is increased after the milestone is confirmed.  When I created a user role ZBR_BPC_EXPERT ahead of time, it only contains the available 109 business catalogs.  This is the root cause of my authorization error when doing work process configurations.

 

Solution

Now we need to update the business role ZBR_BPC_EXPERT.  We do this by using the Fiori App Business Role Templates. Within this app, we select Different From Business Roles in the filter Show Business Role Templates. Then hit Go button. Now we see there are three role templates different from roles copied from them, including template SAP_BR_PBC_EXPERT.

 Find Out Business Role Templates different from Business Roles

By clicking on the arrow at the end of row SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT, we find there are 223 business catalogs. Hooray!

To fix the problem, click on the Business Roles tab.  Here you can see I have three business roles all copied from the same template SAP_BR_PBC_EXPERT, but only one role ZBR_BPC_EXPERT is different from the template. Note: the other two roles were created later after CBC milestone was confirmed.

After selecting the role ZBR_BPC_EXPERT and click on the Compare -> Business Catalogs button, a comparison screen shows up. Most probably you need to scroll down the list to expose those business catalogs not included in the role yet.

 Compare Business Role ZBR_BPC_EXPERT with Its Template

After finding those missing business catalogs, you can individually select those business catalogs you need and then hit the Save button, or just hit the Apply All button to include all available business catalogs.  In my case, I need all of them.

 Add More Business Catalogs to a Business Role

 

Conclusion

From above investigation we learned that not only assigning a business role to a user is important, but “when” the business role was created is also important.  If we create a business role too early before all business catalogs are available, we might not get sufficient authorizations.  However, this problem can be resolved by synchronizing your business roles with their relevant templates.

It also worth pointing out that after a major upgrade, the business catalogs within a business role template might be changed. You need to update the business roles with the latest templates accordingly.

 

References

  • Blog: From A to Z: Setup a Starter System of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition [2024 Edition]
  • Blog: Your Sherlock Holmes - Why a Fiori App Cannot be Found in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

entry.title: Re: Negative cwm stock in return status
entry.content: Sorry Ali, that was too long ago to remember. We've gone to a new ERP since then.

entry.title: Need advice/steps setting upTDE on ASE 16 with 70 dbs mix of rep /non rep dbs refreshed to targets
entry.content: Is there a write up commands to setup Sybase TDE encryption at rest to setup on the prod - DR servers with a mix of replicated and non replicated dbs and a daily refresh to the UAT for selective dbs. For exmple - if I have 70 dbs  on ASE server, do I need to create 70 encryption keys and decrypt/encrypt with every database scheduled daily. Tried following manuals, not getting it right.

entry.title: How to unselect selected rows in list report table
entry.content:
I am using the code below to get the selected rows from my table, it is a fiori elements list report app
 
let aContexts = this.extensionAPI.getSelectedContexts();
 
            for (let i = 0; i < aContexts.length; i++) {
                const element = aContexts[i].getProperty();
 
after backend processing I want to unmark the lines, what should I do?

entry.title: Re: Start Your scripting Journey The Easy Way With SAP Analytics Cloud | Part Three
entry.content:
@tylerrose For almost all my demos I use Canvas rather than Responsive. You can still get this to work on multiple devices etc but for my work Canvas is my go to page type. They will still work with different resolutions, screens etc, you just have to work with %size rather than a specific size.

Daniel.

entry.title: Is bcp in SAP IQ compatible with bcp in SQL server?
entry.content: I am thinking to use bcp to dump the data from SAP IQ and load into SQL Server by bcp.  Is it possible?

entry.title: Re: Start Your scripting Journey The Easy Way With SAP Analytics Cloud | Part Two
entry.content:
@tylerrose 

Thank you for the comments - I think for the next blog (Part #5) I'll look at why my stories look like they do. For me design is a science not art so my dashboards look like the do for good reason. I'm a practitioner of IBCS so each element is considered, each chart chosen to convey a message etc.

Have a look for IBCS on Youtube - some great tips on design, layout and the WHY stories look like they do.

I will document some design tips in my next blog and we'll use this story (below) as the basis for the design.

Look out for the next installment before the end of the year.

Daniel

entry.title: Transport the Initial Business Configuration Content into the test system
entry.content:
Hello , 

We are preparing the test system to initiative our UAT, and I need to push configuration from CBC to TestS/4 PUBLIC CLOUD

in SAP help it says : "select the transport for your initial business configuration content (CBC Staging)."

When I go in the Export Customizing Transport tile in my Customizing tenant, I can't see the transport Request CBC Staging , I can only see "BC Initial Scope ".

I've been looking into an other system and I can see the CBC Staging transport request.

Can you help me with that? Is it ok If I push the BC Initial scope without having the CBC Staging ?

Our version is 2408.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Generating PDF on BTP CF using CAP and Puppeteer
entry.content:
Thanks for sharing.  I've been looking at a couple of options around generating PDFs including this approach here using PDFKit.  So it was nice to find related content here.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-generate-and-download-pdfs-on-the-fly-without-persisting-in-cap/ba-p/13734006  

I actually found a pretty nice post, which compares a few options on PDF generation in JS.

https://dev.to/xeshan6981/the-5-nodejs-pdf-libraries-every-developer-must-know-4b39 

One thing I would ask, is the use of "_" in your handler is private and to my knowledge is subject to change by SAP.  It may be better to tweak the example to follow the approaches defined for serving media in the CAP help.

https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/providing-services#annotating-media-elements

I understand your code is for demonstration purposes, but you'll find newer developers may incorporate this approach somewhere productively or at least highlight this for them so they know.

entry.title: Integration Suite - iFlow Authorization Error
entry.content:
Hi ,
I don't know if the integration suite trial version is facing some issue, because this week I'm receiving the folow ERROR: "You are not authorized to edit the artifact due to access policies. Contact your tenant administrator for access." . If I try to create a new package iFlow, If I try to edit a existing iFlow, always iss the same error.

I tried to activated the Integration suite in another account nad it is not pssobiel ERROR again.

This two operation I tried in different workstation and occurs the same erros.

Is there any problem in SAP side?

Regards ,
Márcio

entry.title: How to update price in Material Master
entry.content:
We would like to know how to change material prices and inventory. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to update price in Material Master
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,


In order to change inventory prices, you can use app "Change Material Prices":
Change Material Prices


To check the PUP valuation (Periodic Unit Price / Moving Average Price), kindly refer to the below documentation:
Valuation with Moving Average Price
2931804 - How to change Periodic Unit Price for V price control material?


Best Regards,
SAP Support
